message:
fix(autograd,cpu): overflow-safe arithmetic hardening

Autograd (ft-autograd/src/lib.rs):
- Roll backward uses rem_euclid() instead of bare % to correctly handle
  negative shift values, which previously caused panics on debug builds
  and silent wraparound on release builds
- Matrix multiply backward (matmul, outer, bmm) now uses
  checked_mul_usize() helper that returns an error instead of panicking
  on dimension overflow, protecting against adversarial tensor shapes
- New normalize_wrapped_index_float() helper centralizes Python-style
  negative indexing with proper bounds checking and NaN rejection
- ensure_tensor_len validation added to catch shape/data length
  mismatches early rather than during gradient computation

CPU kernels (ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs):
- Expanded overflow-safe arithmetic across multiple CPU kernel paths,
  replacing unchecked multiplications with checked variants in:
  - Convolution stride/dilation calculations
  - Pooling window offset computations
  - Reshape dimension inference
  - Index-select gather/scatter offsets
- All overflow paths now return descriptive errors instead of panicking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
